---
title: AWS Bedrock integration rules
scope: project
---

<!-- Generated by `harness rules bootstrap`. Edit to tune; re-run to regenerate. -->

- **Credentials via IAM roles, never static keys.** Authenticate the GitHub Actions runner to Bedrock with OIDC-assumed roles; on Vercel use scoped env-injected role credentials. NEVER hardcode or commit `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`/`AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, and never expose them to the React/client bundle — all Bedrock calls go through server-only code (API routes / LangGraph worker).
- **Least-privilege IAM scoped to ARNs.** Grant only `bedrock:Converse`, `bedrock:ConverseStream`, and (if used) `bedrock:InvokeModel*` / `bedrock:ApplyGuardrail`, with `Resource` pinned to specific foundation-model / inference-profile ARNs and the guardrail ARN — not `*`. Use a separate, narrower role per environment.
- **Call the Converse API, not model-specific InvokeModel payloads.** Use `Converse`/`ConverseStream` (BedrockRuntimeClient) with `modelId`, `system`, `messages`, and `inferenceConfig` so models stay swappable. For throughput/quota resilience, target a cross-region **inference profile ID** rather than a bare model ID.
- **Always attach a published Guardrail.** Pass `guardrailConfig` with `guardrailIdentifier` + an explicit numbered `guardrailVersion` (NEVER `DRAFT`) on every generation call; use `streamProcessingMode: "sync"` when output must be fully screened before display. Use the standalone `ApplyGuardrail` API to screen extracted claims / evidence text without a model call.
- **Guardrails are not the only check.** Keep deterministic policy + claim-provenance checks (per the spec's evidence model) in addition to Guardrails for PII filters, denied topics, and contextual-grounding (unsupported-claim) detection — never treat a Guardrail pass as sufficient.
- **Handle ThrottlingException with backoff.** Enable the SDK `adaptive` retry mode; on `ThrottlingException`/429 and `ServiceQuotaExceededException` retry with exponential backoff + jitter. Treat quotas as per-model/per-region and surface a user-safe "try again" state in the UI rather than crashing the run.
- **Idempotency: synchronous Converse has none — the app must dedupe.** Key each generation node on a stable `(release_run_id, feature_id, skill_snapshot)` id and persist results before retrying so re-runs/retries don't double-generate or double-bill. For batch (`CreateModelInvocationJob`) pass a unique `clientRequestToken` (≤256 chars) so a replayed submit is ignored, not duplicated.
- **Webhooks: Bedrock has no native outbound webhook.** Async/batch job state arrives via EventBridge → (optionally) SNS. On an SNS HTTP(S) endpoint you MUST verify the message signature before processing: prefer `SignatureVersion` 2 (SHA256), fetch the signing cert only over HTTPS from an `sns.<region>.amazonaws.com` host, validate the cert chain, and reject any message whose `TopicArn` is unexpected. Confirm `SubscriptionConfirmation` only after the same validation.
- **Webhook idempotency + auth.** Dedupe inbound events on SNS `MessageId` (or the EventBridge event `id`) and ignore replays. If using an EventBridge API destination instead of SNS, require a shared-secret auth header on the Next.js route and reject requests lacking it.
- **Separate sandbox vs prod.** Use distinct IAM roles, guardrail IDs, SNS topics/secrets, and S3 buckets per environment; never let a sandbox run write prod provenance or invoke prod-only models.
- **Protect data in transit and at rest.** Require TLS 1.2+ to Bedrock; redact PII from evidence BEFORE it enters LangGraph state, Aurora/pgvector, or a Bedrock prompt. Model-invocation logging is opt-in — if enabled, the prompt/output logs land in your S3/CloudWatch, so keep secrets and customer PII out of prompts, tags, and free-text fields.
- **Don't log prompts/outputs/credentials.** Scrub model inputs/outputs from application logs and error envelopes; log a request id + token usage (from the Converse response `usage`) for cost/telemetry instead of raw content.
